titleStmt.title: Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 554

p.seg: I, Duncan, son of King Malcolm, by inheritance undoubted king of Scotland,

have given in alms to Saint Cuthbert and his own servants, Tyninghame, Auldhame, Scoughall, Knowes, Hedderwick, and at Broxmouth all the service that Bishop Fothad had from that place, and I have given these in such quittance with sake and soke, as better than Saint Cuthbert ever had from those of whom he holds his own alms. And I have given this for myself and for my father's soul, and for my brothers, and for my wife, and for my children. And since I have willed that this gift should permanently belong to Saint Cuthbert, I have done what my brothers have allowed.
But let the one who would destroy this thing, or take anything of it away from the servants of Saint Cuthbert, have God’s curse, and Saint Cuthbert’s, and mine. Amen.
The cross of King Dun+can. Of the scribe, Gr+ent. Ach+ard. Ul+f. Malcolm+. Edgar+. Her+mer. Ælf+ric. Uv+iet. Hem+ming. Theo+bald. E+rnulf
